old_claim.note: Municipal Debt Bonds is contextual infrastructure or shared knowledge, not a strict hard prerequisite.

new_claim.note: Taxation and Tribute is the general fiscal mechanism behind murage, tallage, and other local charges used for wall upkeep.

source_shape.source_locator: City of York Council says murage taxes were levied for building and maintaining city defences, and that tallage was charged on royal tenements to provide upkeep income.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source describes tax-based wall upkeep finance, not debt-bond issuance.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Murage and tallage are tax mechanisms; municipal debt bonds are a different public-finance instrument not documented in the York wall-maintenance source.

ontology_before: Town Wall Maintenance Taxes depended on Municipal Debt Bonds, conflating tax and leased-income maintenance funding with debt issuance.
ontology_after: Town Wall Maintenance Taxes depends on Taxation and Tribute for the general tax mechanism behind murage, tallage, and local charges.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: town_wall_maintenance_taxes now depends on taxation_and_tribute and no longer directly depends on municipal_debt_bonds.
would_reject_if: The node were rescoped to Italian municipal bond finance rather than York wall-maintenance taxation.
A stronger source showed York wall upkeep was primarily funded by municipal bond issuance.
The taxation_and_tribute node were narrowed away from local duties, tolls, or charges.
short_reason: York wall upkeep used taxes, not municipal debt bonds.
